arguments.task: Research Stripe using official first-party sources. Open https://stripe.com/docs/api and extract evidence for: one-line API description; authentication methods supported for the Stripe API and how API keys are used; whether access is self-serve or gated, including any pricing or signup requirements for API use; API surface type (REST and/or GraphQL) and breadth from docs/navigation; rate limit notes; and any notable signals such as official SDKs, OpenAPI, API versioning, webhooks, and changelog. Then separately verify whether an official Stripe MCP server exists from Stripe official docs or GitHub. Return concise findings with exact supporting URLs.

input.thought: Start first-party web research on Stripe docs and official properties.

arguments.task: Use official first-party Stripe pages only and directly inspect these URLs: https://docs.stripe.com/api , https://docs.stripe.com/keys , https://docs.stripe.com/rate-limits , https://docs.stripe.com/get-started/account , https://docs.stripe.com/changelog , and if available an official Stripe MCP page or official GitHub repo. Extract exact findings with URLs for: API auth methods, self-serve account creation/access, API surface type and breadth, rate limit specifics, SDKs/webhooks/versioning/changelog, and whether an official MCP exists.

raw_result.description: Stripe provides a REST API for accepting payments, managing financial operations, and integrating billing, payouts, and related commerce workflows.
raw_result.auth_notes: Stripe API docs show requests authenticate with API keys using secret and restricted keys; docs include test/live key formats such as sk_test, rk_live, and sk_live. No OAuth requirement was verified from the gathered evidence for core API access.
raw_result.gating_reason: Stripe provides self-serve account creation and API keys in docs; no partner or contact-sales gate for initial API access was verified from the gathered evidence.

api_surface.breadth: Broad public API reference spanning many resource groups and endpoints for payments, billing, webhooks, and other financial operations; official client libraries are documented for Ruby, Python, PHP, Java, Node.js, Go, and .NET.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Evidence gathered from Stripe docs indicates a Model Context Protocol link/sidebar item exists, suggesting an official Stripe MCP offering, but the exact MCP page and scope were not fully verified in the captured evidence.

raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence indicates Stripe documents rate limits publicly, including a global live limit of 100 operations/sec and sandbox limit of 25 operations/sec, with some endpoint-specific limits; this should be rechecked directly by a human against the current rate-limit page wording.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official first-party API reference, documented API keys, public changelog, documented webhooks, API versioning, and official SDKs for multiple languages are strong signs of mature agent-toolkit readiness.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The existence and exact scope of an official Stripe MCP server/page were only indirectly observed in gathered browser notes, not directly verified from a final captured source passage. Rate-limit numeric details were also obtained from task memory rather than a quoted final extract.
